perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby acknowledges having been advised that: i) BDO Canada Limited (“ BDO”) was appointed as Proposal Trustee of Baldwin on February 14 , 2012 in accordance with the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act . ii) Towards that end, BDO has in its possession certain proprietary, non- public or confidential information, documents, statements, and data relating to the assets of Baldwin (the “ Assets” and “Information ”) for the review and further investigation, where appropriate, of any person or corpor ation interested in acquiring the Assets. iii) The Information is sensitive and confidential in nature, and the disclosure thereof could adversely affect the value of the Assets of Baldwin.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned hereby acknowledges having been advised that: i) BDO Canada Limited (“ BDO”) was appointed as Proposal Trustee of Baldwin on February 14, 2012 in accordance with the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act. ii) Towards that end, BDO has in its possession certain proprietary, non-public or confidential information, documents, statements, and data relating to the financial holdings of Baldwin (the “Holdings” and “Information”) for the review and further investigation, where appropriate, of any person or corporation interested in acquiring the Holdings. iii) The Information is sensitive and confidential in nature, and the disclosure thereof could adversely affect the value of the Assets of Baldwin.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Assets' as the assets of Baldwin. The changed text redefines 'Assets' as the 'financial holdings' of Baldwin when introducing the term 'Holdings', but then in the third point, refers to 'Assets' instead of 'Holdings'. This creates an ambiguity in whether the confidential information relates to all the assets or only the financial holdings and contradicts itself by switching between the definitions in the same paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned hereby acknowledges having been advised that: i) BDO Canada Limited (“ BDO”) was appointed as Proposal Trustee of Baldwin on February 14, 2012 in accordance with the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act. ii) Towards that end, BDO has in its possession certain proprietary, non-public or confidential information, documents, statements, and data relating to the financial holdings of Baldwin (the “Holdings” and “Information”) for the review and further investigation, where appropriate, of any person or corporation interested in acquiring the Holdings. iii) The Information is sensitive and confidential in nature, and the disclosure thereof could adversely affect the value of the Assets of Baldwin.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Assets' as 'Holdings', leading to a contradiction in the third point where 'Assets' is used instead of 'Holdings'. This creates an ambiguity as the text uses both definitions in the same context in both the changed and contradicted text. The original text does not have this ambiguity, as 'Assets' is consistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: To maintain the Information in the strictest of confidence and to control the dissemination of the Information, including any documents or copies (paper, electronic or otherwise) thereof contained in the Information in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality and Non- Disclosure Agreement (“ NDA”);
perturbation.changed_text: To maintain the Data in the strictest of confidence and to control the dissemination of the Information, including any documents or copies (paper, electronic or otherwise) thereof contained in the Information in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement (“ NDA”);
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to maintaining 'Information' in confidence. The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data' in the first instance, but continues to use 'Information' afterwards in the same section. This causes confusion as to whether all aspects of the agreement regarding confidentiality apply to Data, Information, or both, particularly since 'Data' is not defined, creating in-text contradiction due to undefined term in place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To maintain the Data in the strictest of confidence and to control the dissemination of the Information, including any documents or copies (paper, electronic or otherwise) thereof contained in the Information in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement (“ NDA”);
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Information' with 'Data' at the beginning of the clause. However, the clause continues to use 'Information' later. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The Perturbation Explanation details that 'Data' is not defined, and the change introduces confusion about whether the confidentiality agreement applies to 'Data', 'Information' or both, thus creating a contradiction. The changed text causes contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: To advise the Representatives of the confidential nature of the Information, and to provide to those Representatives to which or to whom the Information is provided a Confidentiality and Non -Disclosure Agreement Baldwin Paper Co. Limited /Papiers Baldwin Compagine Limitee and 9169- 2707 Quebec Inc. o/a Papiers Baldwin – February 2012 2 of 4 copy of the NDA, and if such Representative is not otherwise bound by restrictions on disclosure and use similar to the obligations hereunder, to have such Representatives agree to be bound by this NDA ;
perturbation.changed_text: To advise the Representatives of the confidential nature of the Data, and to provide to those Representatives to which or to whom the Information is provided a Confidentiality and Non -Disclosure Agreement Baldwin Paper Co. Limited /Papiers Baldwin Compagine Limitee and 9169- 2707 Quebec Inc. o/a Papiers Baldwin – February 2012 2 of 4 copy of the NDA, and if such Representative is not otherwise bound by restrictions on disclosure and use similar to the obligations hereunder, to have such Representatives agree to be bound by this NDA ;
perturbation.explanation: The original text speaks of the confidential nature of 'Information'. The changed text introduces the term 'Data' instead of Information, while still using the term 'Information' later in the same sentence. It is unclear whether the representatives must be informed about the confidential nature of just 'Data' or both 'Data' and 'Information', creating confusion and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To advise the Representatives of the confidential nature of the Data, and to provide to those Representatives to which or to whom the Information is provided a Confidentiality and Non -Disclosure Agreement Baldwin Paper Co. Limited /Papiers Baldwin Compagine Limitee and 9169- 2707 Quebec Inc. o/a Papiers Baldwin – February 2012 2 of 4 copy of the NDA, and if such Representative is not otherwise bound by restrictions on disclosure and use similar to the obligations hereunder, to have such Representatives agree to be bound by this NDA ;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data' in the first instance, creating a contradiction. The sentence then refers to 'Information', creating confusion about whether 'Data' and 'Information' are distinct terms or if they are interchangeable. Because the contradicted text and changed text are in the same location it doesn't change the fact that there is now an in-text contradiction by way of misaligned terminology. This creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned shall indemnify BDO , Baldwin and their representatives against any losses, cost s, damage s, expense s or liabilities suffered or incurred by any of them as a result of or in connection with any breach by the undersigned or any of its Representatives to whom the undersigned discloses Information of any term or provision of this NDA; prov ided, however, that the undersigned shall not be liable for any consequential, special, incidental, indirect or penal damages;
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned shall indemnify BDO , Baldwin and their representatives against any losses, cost s, damage s, expense s or liabilities suffered or incurred by any of them as a result of or in connection with any breach by the undersigned or any of its Representatives to whom the undersigned discloses Records of any term or provision of this NDA; prov ided, however, that the undersigned shall not be liable for any consequential, special, incidental, indirect or penal damages;
perturbation.explanation: The original text mentions indemnification for breaches relating to disclosure of 'Information.' The changed text refers to disclosure of 'Records.' This creates an ambiguity: is the indemnification triggered by disclosing all Information, or only Records? The change creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned shall indemnify BDO , Baldwin and their representatives against any losses, cost s, damage s, expense s or liabilities suffered or incurred by any of them as a result of or in connection with any breach by the undersigned or any of its Representatives to whom the undersigned discloses Records of any term or provision of this NDA; prov ided, however, that the undersigned shall not be liable for any consequential, special, incidental, indirect or penal damages;
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to indemnification for breaches related to 'Information' disclosure. The changed text narrows this to 'Records.' The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, now only triggers indemnification for 'Records'. This creates a contradiction because if a party discloses 'Information' that isn't considered a 'Record', it is unclear whether indemnification applies, thereby contradicting the original broader scope and creating ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this NDA shall expire , as it relates only to the Information associated with the Assets or parts thereof purchased or otherwise acquired by the undersigned, upon the completion of a Definitive Agreement . In all other cases, the terms of this NDA shall expire one (1) year after the completion of a sale or other transfer of the Assets.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this NDA shall expire , as it relates only to the Records associated with the Holdings or parts thereof purchased or otherwise acquired by the undersigned, upon the completion of a Definitive Agreement . In all other cases, the terms of this NDA shall expire one (1) year after the completion of a sale or other transfer of the Assets.
perturbation.explanation: The original text has NDA expiration related to the 'Information' and 'Assets'. By changing it to 'Records' and 'Holdings' respectively, but leaving 'Assets' later in the same sentence, the expiration clause becomes confusing. Does the NDA expire for Records associated with the Holdings purchased? Or does it expire after one year regarding the sale of the Assets? This creates in-text contradiction and is unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of this NDA shall expire , as it relates only to the Records associated with the Holdings or parts thereof purchased or otherwise acquired by the undersigned, upon the completion of a Definitive Agreement . In all other cases, the terms of this NDA shall expire one (1) year after the completion of a sale or other transfer of the Assets.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Information' to 'Records' and 'Assets' to 'Holdings' in the initial clause regarding NDA expiration upon a Definitive Agreement. However, it retains 'Assets' in the subsequent clause concerning NDA expiration one year after a sale or transfer. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the one-year expiration applies to the sale of 'Assets' as initially stated, or if it only relates to 'Records associated with the Holdings' purchased, or even to both. The contradiction is strong because it involves the core element of the contract, when the NDA expires, and the change directly conflicts with itself.
